rationale: The cited paper is used in the analysis strategy, not just as background. In Study 2, the citing paper explicitly uses the Johnson-Neyman procedure and cites Spiller et al. (2013) to determine the moderator-value regions where the centrality effect is significant. This citation directly supports how the interaction is probed and interpreted in the results section, giving it a clear functional role.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Spiller et al. (2013)'s moderated regression probing guidance, specifically Johnson-Neyman floodlight testing, to interpret an interaction in Study 2. It uses that approach to identify the D-score ranges where spatial centrality significantly changes risk preference.
